name: Dolce Torino/Torta Gianduja
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup flour plus 3/4 cup cake flour (or use 1 1/2 cups minus 1 1/2 tbsp flour)
recipeInstructions:
1. Toast the hazelnuts in the oven and remove the outer skins.
2. Melt 1 tbsp of sugar in a small saucepan and add the toasted hazelnuts, stirring until smooth to form a hazelnut paste.
3. Melt 3 oz (85 g) of unsweetened chocolate and set aside.
4. Whisk together 6 eggs, 3 egg yolks, and all but 1 tbsp of the remaining sugar in a bowl standing over warm water; do not allow the water to exceed body temperature. Add honey, remove from heat, and continue beating until cool and thickened.
5. Mix melted butter, chocolate, vanilla, and hazelnut paste in another bowl over hot water or double boiler until smooth.
6. Sift flour and cake flour into the egg mixture and fold gently. Fold in the chocolate-hazelnut mixture, then pour into two buttered and floured 9-inch/23 cm cake pans.
7. Bake at 190°C (375°F) for about 40 minutes. Remove from oven and turn onto a clean cloth to cool.
8. Melt remaining unsweetened chocolate with cream in hot water or double boiler until smooth, stirring occasionally to avoid skin formation.
9. Melt apricot jam and remaining sugar in saucepan over low heat until melted; set aside.
10. Melt sweetened chocolate and set aside.
11. Whip cream and chocolate mixture until stiff.
12. Place cooled cakes on serving platter with wax paper underneath. Remove crust, spread liqueur-brandy mixture, then spread chocolate cream, chocolate, and apricot jam over one cake, stacking both cakes. Coat top with remaining liqueur and apricot jam before serving.
13. Serve at room temperature in a cool place; do not refrigerate.
This traditional Gianduja cake is often baked in a round pan with "Gianduja" or "Gianduia" written across it using melted chocolate. If unsweetened chocolate is unavailable, substitute bittersweet and reduce sugar to about 3/4 cup (half the suggested amount).
